headline: Sky-high funding rates signal overcrowded bullish bets
detail: Elevated funding rates mean traders are paying a premium to stay long, which historically precedes sharp mean-reversion flushes. For a book that is 90% tech and chip names on margin, a sudden unwind would hit hardest in exactly your names - SOXL, SMH, CRWD. This is the single most relevant macro signal for your portfolio today.
rec: Consider trimming SOXL toward a smaller size before your stop gets hit - you're already down 14.5% and a flush driven by long liquidations could gap through $200. No new buys are warranted in this environment.

headline: Fund manager bought Nvidia and SK Hynix, sold software - follow the money in AI
detail: The thesis here is hardware over software for AI monetization right now - chips and memory, not SaaS. That directionally supports SMH and SOXL as the right sector, but it does not fix the near-term technical damage or the funding-rate warning. TSM is on the scanner as an Accumulate with a 7/12 score, grade B bias.
rec: TSM scanner entry is $443.34-$443.79 with a stop at $442.47 - worth watching as a cleaner, non-leveraged way to play the hardware AI theme, but only if the broader chip tape stabilizes. Not a buy today given macro noise.

headline: Meta pushing into cloud - Wall Street bracing for margin compression
detail: Meta building cloud infrastructure means more capex, lower near-term margins - this is a read-across for the whole hyperscaler ecosystem. For your names it matters indirectly: if cloud margin compression spreads as a narrative, it could weigh on software and broad tech sentiment, dragging QQQ and SPY.
rec: No immediate action needed - your QQQ and SPY stops are well below current prices at $670 and $705 respectively. Just be aware this narrative could build and pressure your index hedges if it widens.

headline: Small-cap trade coming to life
detail: Three factors reportedly driving small-caps higher is a rotation signal - money moving from large-cap tech into small-caps would directly reduce demand for your core names. This is a tail risk for a 90% tech/chip book, not a crisis today, but worth monitoring as a rotation flag.
rec: No action needed today, but if QQQ starts lagging while IWM accelerates, that is your cue to reduce overall tech exposure. Watch QQQ relative strength over the next few sessions.
